components.name: DataGrid
components.description: A powerful data grid component with sorting, filtering, pagination, row selection, column visibility toggling, and built-in CRUD actions (View/Edit/Delete). Supports both client-side and server-side pagination.

props.name: columns
props.description: Array of column definitions defining how each column renders

props.name: data
props.description: Array of data items to display in the grid

props.name: rowKey
props.description: Property name or function to get unique row identifier

props.name: filterable
props.description: Array of column keys that should have filter inputs

props.name: selectable
props.description: Whether rows can be selected via checkboxes

props.name: selected
props.description: Controlled selected row keys (used with onSelectChange)

props.name: onSelectChange
props.description: Callback when selection changes

props.name: pageSize
props.description: Number of items per page

props.name: showPagination
props.description: Whether to show pagination controls

props.name: showColumnToggle
props.description: Whether to show column visibility toggle

props.name: loading
props.description: Whether to show loading skeleton

props.name: emptyMessage
props.description: Message shown when no data is available

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS classes for the container

props.name: onRowClick
props.description: Callback when a row is clicked

props.name: defaultActions
props.description: Configuration for default row actions (view/edit/delete). See DefaultActionsConfig for details.

props.name: serverPagination
props.description: Server-side pagination prop (for controlled/server-fetched pagination)

components.example:
<DataGrid
  columns={[
    { key: 'id', header: 'ID', sortable: true },
    { key: 'name', header: 'Name', sortable: true },
    { key: 'email', header: 'Email' }
  ]}
  data={users}
  rowKey='id'
  filterable={['name', 'email']}
  selectable
  pageSize={25}
  onRowClick={(row) => console.log(row)}
/>

components.name: useServerDataGrid
components.description: Custom hook for fetching and managing server-side paginated data grid. Automatically handles Laravel encryption, column derivation, and pagination state.

props.name: url
props.description: API endpoint URL to fetch data from. Page parameter is appended automatically (e.g., /api/users?page=1)

props.name: params
props.description: Query parameters to include in the request

props.name: encrypt
props.description: If true, expects Laravel-encrypted payload and decrypts it automatically

props.name: key
props.description: Laravel APP_KEY used for decryption. Pass import.meta.env['VITE_LARAVEL_KEY']

props.name: decryptPayloadLog
props.description: If true, logs the decrypted payload to the console

props.name: columnOverrides
props.description: Override auto-derived column definitions per key. Example: { status: { render: (row) => <Badge>{row.status}</Badge> }, score: { sortable: true } }

components.name: DataGridColumn
components.description: Type definition for DataGrid column configuration

props.name: key
props.description: Unique key identifying this column (must match a property in T)

props.name: header
props.description: Column header display text or React node

props.name: render
props.description: Custom render function for cell content

props.name: sortable
props.description: Whether this column is sortable

props.name: width
props.description: Column width (e.g., '100px', '20%')

props.name: align
props.description: Text alignment within the column

components.example: { key: 'name', header: 'Name', sortable: true, width: '200px', align: 'left' }

components.name: ServerDataGridProp
components.description: Type definition for server-side pagination in DataGrid

props.name: pagination
props.description: Server pagination metadata from Laravel/API

props.name: currentPage
props.description: Current active page number (1-based)

props.name: goToPage
props.description: Function to navigate to a specific page

components.example: { pagination: { total: 100, per_page: 10, last_page: 10 }, currentPage: 1, goToPage: (p) => setPage(p) }
